representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure is designed to resolve policy uncertainty by generating interpretations and gathering relevant inputs to assess them. If the assessment indicates insufficient acceptability, the process loops back to repeat these steps, ending only when an interpretation is successfully accepted or when continued review is no longer justified.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: Identify policy uncertainty -> Generate candidate interpretations -> Gather review inputs -> Assess interpretations -> (Loop if insufficient acceptability) -> Terminate upon acceptance or lack of justification for further review.

ambiguities.location: The process loops if acceptability remains insufficient.
ambiguities.description: It is not explicitly stated which exact point of the procedure the loop returns to (e.g., does it regenerate candidates, regather inputs, or only reassess?).
plausible_interpretations: Loop returns to generating candidate interpretations.
Loop returns to gathering inputs based on existing candidates.
Loop returns only to assessing current interpretations.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects the formal transition targets of the loop structure within the primitive layer.

recognition_analysis.recognition_basis: The procedure describes a classic policy analysis or exception handling workflow, particularly matching systems of compliance or statutory interpretation.
recognition_analysis.prior_knowledge_leakage_risk: Low. The extracted structure is maintained strictly within the bounds of the provided 6 steps without borrowing external governance framework details.
